nodes.description: George Washington inherited his first slaves from his father at the age of 11 and continued to acquire more throughout his life, owning over 300 slaves at his death.

nodes.title: [2023-03-19] George Washington began to question the morality of slavery during his lifetime
nodes.description: Throughout his life, George Washington started to question the morality of slavery and freed some of his slaves in his will.

nodes.title: [2023-03-19] George Washington did not free all of his slaves due to economic concerns
nodes.description: George Washington did not free all of his slaves when he died because it was common for slave-owning plantation owners to keep slaves as property and pass them down to heirs, and he was concerned about the economic loss from freeing them.

nodes.title: [2023-03-19] George Washington's will provided for the emancipation of certain slaves
nodes.description: In his will, George Washington freed some of his slaves and provided for the emancipation of any enslaved person who had served him for at least 30 years or who was above the age of 60, with conditions for service until certain ages.

nodes.title: [2023-03-19] George Washington's will specified service conditions for freed slaves
nodes.description: George Washington's will stated that the slaves he freed were to be 'bound to service' until the age of 26 for males and the age of 24 for females.

nodes.title: [2023-03-19] George Washington left 123 slaves in the custody of his wife Martha
nodes.description: George Washington left 123 slaves in the custody of his wife Martha and instructed her to provide for them, but not to free them.

nodes.title: [2023-03-19] Martha Washington passed away in 1802
nodes.description: Martha Washington passed away in 1802, and at that time, many states had laws requiring freed slaves to leave the state within a certain period of time or risk re-enslavement.

nodes.title: [2023-03-19] Martha Washington may have kept some slaves to protect them from re-enslavement
nodes.description: It is possible that Martha Washington kept some slaves inherited from her husband to protect them from re-enslavement due to laws requiring freed slaves to leave the state.

nodes.title: [2023-03-19] Martha Washington's intentions for keeping slaves are unclear
nodes.description: It is not clear what Martha Washington's intentions were for keeping some of the slaves inherited from her husband, as owning slaves was common practice at the time and economic reasons may have influenced her decision.

nodes.title: [2023-04-08] Procter & Gamble introduced 'Leadership Blueprint' performance evaluation system

nodes.title: [2023-04-08] Toyota uses 'Hoshin Kanri' performance evaluation system
nodes.description: Toyota employs a performance evaluation system called 'Hoshin Kanri' which involves setting overarching strategic goals for the company and cascading those goals down to each department and individual employee, improving efficiency and productivity.

nodes.title: [2023-04-08] Criticism of performance evaluation systems includes unintended consequences
nodes.description: Common critiques of performance evaluation systems include unintended consequences such as discouraging collaboration, discouraging risk-taking, or encouraging employees to 'game the system' to achieve higher ratings.

nodes.title: [2023-04-08] Criticism of performance evaluation systems includes biases
nodes.description: Performance evaluation systems can be biased, either intentionally or unintentionally, with managers potentially unfairly rating employees based on gender, race, age, or other characteristics.

nodes.title: [2023-04-08] Criticism of performance evaluation systems includes lack of transparency
nodes.description: Employees may feel that the evaluation process is opaque, making it difficult to understand how they are being judged or what they need to do to improve.

nodes.title: [2023-04-08] Criticism of performance evaluation systems includes overemphasis on metrics
nodes.description: When evaluation systems focus too much on metrics and quantitative measures, employees may prioritize achieving those measures at the expense of other important aspects of their work.

nodes.title: [2023-04-08] Performance evaluation systems can drive positive change in organizations
nodes.description: Despite criticisms, performance evaluation systems can be effective tools for driving positive change in organizations when used as part of a larger strategy that includes frequent feedback, coaching, and employee development.

nodes.description: Module 5 of the curriculum covers understanding principles of interaction design, creating interactive prototypes, animation and motion design, micro-interactions, and designing for different devices and platforms.

nodes.description: Module 6 of the curriculum covers understanding design software such as Sketch, Figma, Adobe XD, learning design best practices, creating and managing design systems, and collaboration and communication tools.

nodes.title: [2023-04-14] Digital product design curriculum includes Designing for Business

nodes.title: [2023-04-14] Digital product design curriculum includes Professional Development

nodes.title: [2023-04-14] St. Nicholas is celebrated on December 6th in Germany as Nikolaus
nodes.description: In Germany, St. Nicholas is celebrated on December 6th, known as 'Nikolaus,' where children leave their shoes out to receive sweets and small gifts from him.

nodes.title: [2023-04-14] St. Nicholas is celebrated on December 6th in Austria with Krampus
nodes.description: In Austria, St. Nicholas is celebrated on December 6th, and he is often accompanied by Krampus, who punishes naughty children.

nodes.title: [2023-04-14] St. Nicholas is celebrated on December 6th in Italy as San Nicola
nodes.description: In Italy, St. Nicholas is celebrated on December 6th, known as 'San Nicola,' where children receive sweets and small gifts from him, and in some regions, parents may hide a small gift in a child's shoe.

nodes.title: [2023-04-14] St. Nicholas is celebrated on December 19th in Russia as Nikolai Chudotvorets
nodes.description: In Russia, St. Nicholas is celebrated on December 19th, known as 'Nikolai Chudotvorets,' with traditional foods, singing, and the giving of gifts.

nodes.title: [2023-04-14] Krampus is a separate figure who punishes naughty children in Austria and Germany
nodes.description: In Austria and Germany, Krampus is a separate figure who appears alongside St. Nicholas, punishing naughty children with a whip made of birch branches, serving as a warning to children to behave well.

nodes.title: [2023-04-14] Krampus adds dark humor to St. Nicholas celebrations in Austria and Germany
nodes.description: The figure of Krampus in Austria and Germany adds an element of dark humor and edginess to the otherwise wholesome and family-friendly St. Nicholas celebrations, highlighting cultural nuances within holiday traditions.

nodes.title: [2023-04-14] User expressed concern about Krampus punishing naughty children
nodes.description: User questioned the harshness of Krampus punishing naughty children, suggesting that it may be unfair if children are just having a bad day.

nodes.title: [2023-04-14] User prefers understanding and compassion in discipline over punishment
nodes.description: User believes that it is important for parents and caregivers to approach discipline with understanding and compassion rather than punishment or shame.
